items.title: Qwen 3.8 enters preview
items.oneliner: Alibaba previewed 2.4T-parameter Qwen 3.8 and promised open weights soon. Frontier competition is no longer a closed club.

items.title: Kimi K3 resets coding economics
items.oneliner: Kimi K3 is reportedly near-Claude quality at far lower cost. Pricing is becoming a model capability, not a billing detail.

items.title: Voice AI shrinks below 500KB
items.oneliner: Moonshine Micro packs VAD, ASR, and TTS into under 500KB. On-device voice just got realistic for cheap hardware.

items.title: WordPress fixes pre-auth RCE
items.oneliner: A pre-auth WordPress core RCE can hit 500 million sites. Patch now, because botnets will not wait for maintenance windows.

items.title: New York targets AI listing fraud
items.oneliner: NYC may force landlords to disclose AI-generated listing images. Synthetic media regulation is moving from theory to enforcement.

items.title: Supabase adds searchable encryption
items.oneliner: Supabase integrated searchable field-level encryption with CipherStash. Teams can finally protect sensitive columns without giving up queries.

items.title: Apple hunts AI chip targets
items.oneliner: Apple is reportedly eyeing AI chip acquisitions after server delays. Owning inference hardware is becoming a strategic necessity.

items.title: AMD's MI400 details surface
items.oneliner: LLVM clues suggest AMD's MI400 ML GPU shifts to Wave32-only execution. Compiler assumptions may need updating before hardware ships.

items.title: Claude Code swaps in Rust Bun
items.oneliner: Claude Code now uses Bun's Rust runtime and claims 10% faster Linux performance. Agent tooling is getting serious about systems engineering.

items.title: Alibaba forks Triton for SAIL
items.oneliner: Alibaba published a Triton fork for SAIL PPUs. Custom accelerator stacks are fragmenting the kernel ecosystem around AI hardware.
